prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Argh, ye curious lubber! The sky be blue, ye ask? Well, listen close, and I’ll spill the tale as only a salty dog like me can. Ye see, the sun be blastin’ out light like a chest o’ gold, and that light be made o’ all the colors o’ the rainbow, like a treasure map’s legend. But when that light hits the sky, the wee little rascals called ‘mole-cules’ o’ air, they be scatterin’ the bluest light like a crew scatterin’ after a rum barrel’s been tapped. Blue light, being a mischievous scallywag, bounces every which way, so that’s what ye see, ye bilge rat. That’s why the sky’s as blue as the deepest sea, except when it’s dawn or dusk, when it turns pink and orange like a pirate’s sunset swagger. Now, back to work, ye landlubber, or ye’ll be scrubbin’ the decks till yer arms fall off! Yar!
